calendar_events.title: 24rd Anniversary Memorial of September 11, 2001
calendar_events.description: On this twenty-fourth anniversary of the terrorist attacks at the World Trade Center, the USS Midway Museum pays tribute and honors our fallen heroes and keep our promise to “NEVER FORGET”.

calendar_events.title: TEDx_AFC
calendar_events.description: In The spirit of Ideas worth spreading, TED has created a program called TEDx. TEDx is a program of local, self-organized events that bring people together to share a TED-like experience. To register for the TEDxAFC: http://tedxafc2013.eventbrite.com/

calendar_events.title: Elemental
calendar_events.description: Biomimicry San Diego is facilitating the only screening of the documentary Elemental in San Diego. calendar_events.title: e-Sports & e-Sports Jr + STEAM Tech Adventure Camps
calendar_events.description: Bring excitement, intrigue, and community to your kid's summer with Hands-On Technology's eSports Tech Adventure Camps! Our eSports camps will dazzle your summer camper by introducing them to the world of eSports gaming in a fun & inclusive environment!!!

calendar_events.title: Super STEAM Adventure Camp
calendar_events.description: A super rotation of our favorite STEM projects all in one camp! Campers experience Robotics, Game Design, Circuits, SnapRovers & more as we rotate tech toys and tech topics throughout an exciting, fast-paced week of camp.

calendar_events.title: Game-gineers: Play-Build-Code Adventure Camps

calendar_events.description: Did you know that outdoor play has long-lasting benefits for both children and adults? Join us this month for Talls and Smalls, an outdoor adventure in the garden for ages 2-5 with an accompanying adult! Come spend quality time in nature with your little one while building a love for our environment and adventurous eating habits.

calendar_events.title: Jewish Studio Project
calendar_events.description: Jewish Studio Project at Coastal Roots Farm Join us for a guided experience with the Jewish Studio Process—an approach that combines Jewish learning, creative exploration, and personal reflection. Through text, conversation, and hands-on materials, we’ll open to a deeper encounter with beauty: not just how things appear, but how they move us, stir us, call us closer to what matters. No artistic experience is needed—just a spirit of curiosity and a willingness to play.

calendar_events.description: Join us for an morning of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Together, we’ll prepare a Farm-Fresh menu using ingredients harvested straight from the Farm. Along the way, we’ll explore the Jewish values that guide what and how we grow, deepening our connection to the land and our food. No prior cooking experience is needed—just a love for good food and great company.

calendar_events.title: Tu B’Shvat Food Forest Festival 2024
calendar_events.description: Tu B'Shvat Food Forest Festival Discover the enchanting world of interconnectedness at Coastal Roots Farm's 9th Annual Tu B'Shvat Food Forest Festival. Jewish tradition teaches that everything exists in relationship: we don’t exist in isolation but rather as part of a larger, interconnected whole. This year’s theme “Together we Grow” reminds us of the profound power of this connection.

calendar_events.description: We are piloting weekly Yoga on the Farm starting Friday, January 17th. Kickoff your weekend with Friday afternoon yoga and meditation on the Farm! Class will feature 40 minutes of gentle yoga from guest teachers Dani Harris & Kali Bolla and 20 minutes of guided meditation by Director of Jewish Life Rebecca Joy Fletcher. Class will be framed by Jewish wisdom. with time to share and ask questions. People of any or no faith background are always welcome.

calendar_events.title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms
calendar_events.description: Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms Following the success of a fall class, guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman returns to the Farm for a joyful, cross-cultural exploration of Qigong & the Poetry of Tehillim— Psalms in Hebrew. In this six-part series, we will bring together two rich traditions in order to enhance physical and spiritual health. With its gentle movements and guided breathing, Qigong will help us to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies.

calendar_events.title: Havdalah on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Havdalah on the Farm Havdalah, literally meaning separation, is a ritual held at the end of Shabbat, after the sun has set and when three stars appear. There are blessings over wine, a candle, and spices. It is a ritual that allows us to hold onto the sweet moments of Shabbat for just a few more minutes before going into our busy week. Join Coastal Roots Farm and The Hive on Saturday, February 19th from 7 - 8:30 PM on the Farm for a special Havdalah service led by Sharone Oren and Ashly Tamayo.

calendar_events.title: Winter Gardening Workshop
calendar_events.description: Join us for a special Winter Gardening Workshop: Tree Pruning. Learn with Christina Cranwell, our experienced Outdoor Educator who has been farming 5+ years across multiple states. There is no experience necessary to join, gloves and materials provided. Cover all the basics of tree maintenance to promote healthy tree form, fruiting, and learn how to choose the right pruning tool to protect the longevity of your fruit tree.

calendar_events.title: Shushan on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Sunday, February 26th Shows at 2pm & 4pm $18/person and kids (18 and under) are free. Join The Hive, Coastal Roots Farm, and Lipinsky Family San Diego Jewish Arts Festival for an experiential theatrical walking tour through the city of Shushan – filled with live dance, music, and merriment as we bring the ancient voices of the Purim story to life. Don’t miss an entertaining afternoon, including original performances from San Diego Ballet and vignettes curated by the San Diego Repertory Theatre.

calendar_events.description: On March 30, 2026, the USS Midway Museum will pay tribute to all U.S. military veterans who served during the Vietnam War.

calendar_events.description: The USS Midway Museum honors U.S. Firefighters and Police Officers with free admission in April.

calendar_events.title: Pesach on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Coastal Roots Farm invites people of all backgrounds, especially young families, to Pesach on the Farm. Great for kiddos up to age 8. Join us Sunday April 2nd from 9:45-11:45am to celebrate Pesach (Passover) with hands-on gardening, flower planting, and a special seder plate scavenger hunt. In the celebration of freedom, enjoy music and movement as we sing and dance in the great outdoors. Tickets are a suggested donation of $18/child, $9/adult. Pre-registration required. Click Here to Register!

calendar_events.title: Yoga and Meditation
calendar_events.description: Kickoff your weekend with Friday afternoon yoga and meditation on the Farm! Class will feature 40 minutes of gentle yoga from a guest teacher and 20 minutes of guided meditation by Director of Jewish Life Rebecca Joy Fletcher. Class will be framed by Jewish wisdom. with time to share and ask questions. People of any or no faith background are always welcome. Please bring your own mat, water bottle, and meditation cushion if you use one.

calendar_events.title: Intergenerational Passover Celebration
calendar_events.description: Join us as we prepare for Passover through learning, crafting, and culinary creativity! In this intergenerational gathering, we’ll explore the meaning of the holiday, make a special Passover treat from scratch, and create a festive craft to decorate your space. With music, nosh, and engaging activities, adults and families with kids of all ages will have the opportunity to connect—with each other, with Jewish tradition, and with the spirit of the holiday in a fun and meaningful way.

Guided by guest artist Suchita Rathi Jhawar and our knowledgeable Farm Educators, you’ll create your own painting while learning about peleh—the Jewish value of wonder.

calendar_events.description: Spend the night on the longest-serving aircraft carrier of the 20th century!

calendar_events.title: Monthly Farm Tour
calendar_events.description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm. On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.

calendar_events.title: Farm-to-Table Dinner – with Menu by Lana
calendar_events.description: Celebrate a Season of Gratitude at Our April Farm-to-Table Dinner Join us for a special evening honoring Javier Guerrero’s nearly eight years of leadership at Coastal Roots Farm. Under Javier’s guidance, the Farm has grown into a thriving, mission-driven community—expanding access to fresh food, deepening educational impact, and cultivating connection through land and tradition. This gathering offers a meaningful opportunity to celebrate his legacy and the community he helped build.

calendar_events.title: Slow Down Swap Party at the Rancho Bernardo Earth Day Fair
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego for a FREE clothing swap at the Rancho Bernardo Community Council's 10th Annual Earth Day Fair! Let’s slow down our consumption, slow down for the day, and exchange some clothing and accessories. Join us in reducing our textile waste to celebrate Earth Day alongside amazing vendors, local organizations, and our community! Event date: Sunday, April 12th Location: Webb Park Swap Event time: 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM A Few Reminders: Please register for the event.

calendar_events.description: Sandra âSandyâ Scheller is an American Holocaust educator, curator, and community advocate based in San Diego, California. She is the founder and curator of âRUTH: Remember Us The Holocaust,â a traveling educational exhibit dedicated to preserving the stories of Holocaust survivors who settled in San Diego County. Schellerâs work focuses on ensuring that the voices of Holocaust survivors continue to be heard as their generation diminishes.

calendar_events.title: Bio Blitz on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Join the search for hidden species on the Farm! A bio blitz is an event where people gather to identify and record as many species of plants and animals as possible within an area. This citizen-science initiative will help the farm team better understand, protect, and promote ecological diversity on the Farm. Join us on April 18th from 2:00 pm to 4:00 pm, for our first Bio Blitz on the farm. No experience is necessary to participate, children and families are welcome.

calendar_events.title: City of Carlsbad’s Earth Month Celebration
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego for a day of family-friendly and sustainable activities at the City of Carlsbad’s Earth Month Celebration, "Love Our Planet: There’s No Place Like Earth." We will be having a clothing swap, DIY sustainable cleaning starter kits, as well as a community mural pop-up! Clothing Swap- Bring in your gently-used clothing and “shop” for free items that others have donated—no money exchanged! It’s a great way to promote sustainability, reduce textile waste, and give clothes...

calendar_events.title: Rainwater Harvesting Workshop
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: I Love A Clean San Diego regularly hosts rainwater harvesting workshops on behalf of the County of San Diego's Waterscape Rebate Program (WRP). The workshops will teach you how capturing rainwater can help you lower your water bill, and how to do it! Upcoming rainwater harvesting workshops will be listed below. Please check back on other workshop dates or contact info@waterscaperebates.com to learn more about our workshops.
calendar_events.location: Spring Valley Community Church, Spring Valley

calendar_events.title: City of La Mesa’s Earth Day Fair
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego for a free clothing swap during the City of La Mesa’s Earth Day Fair ! Celebrate the beauty and abundance of our planet and stop by our clothing and accessories swap. Enjoy a day at MacArthur Park, slow down the consumption of new items, and exchange your clothing, shoes, and accessories you want to pass on to another home to keep our fabrics out of the landfill.
calendar_events.location: La Mesa Community Garden, La Mesa, CA

calendar_events.title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special.

calendar_events.title: Community Kayak Cleanup
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us as we clean up our wetlands! Help keep our local waters beautiful and thriving by joining a kayak-based cleanup adventure! Paddle through coastal habitats, collect litter, and protect marine life while enjoying the beauty of the bay. No prior experience needed—just bring your enthusiasm, and we’ll provide the equipment and guidance. Every piece of trash you collect helps create a cleaner, healthier environment for our coastal communities.

calendar_events.title: From Soil to Soul: Adult Farm Camp

Immerse yourself in regenerative agriculture, farm-fresh food, nature-inspired art, earth-based Judaism, climate education, and the invitation to make new friends. The day will contain opportunities to put your hands in the dirt, make art, harvest and cook, and find joy in embodied Jewish wisdom. 🗓️ Sunday, April 19 ⏰ 9:30 am - 3:30 pm. 🎟️ $75/person Pricing Includes all materials, lunch, drinks, and snacks.

calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Learn to reduce your food waste and compost at home! Join I Love A Clean San Diego for our free Transform Food Waste with Vermicomposting Workshop in the Old Poway Park’s Great room on Sunday, April 19 th at 10:30am. During this workshop, we’ll cover: Ways to reduce food waste in the kitchen Discuss the importance of keeping food waste out of landfills Explain how red wigglers transform food waste into fresh compost How to properly utilize the organic waste recycling program with your green bin...

Please bring your own mat, water bottle, and meditation cushion if you use one.

calendar_events.title: 24th Annual Creek to Bay Cleanup Day
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego to be a part of the largest Earth Week cleanup in all of San Diego county! Volunteers are invited to grab buckets and work gloves and make a difference! This year, our countywide Creek to Bay will have around 100+ locations for volunteers to choose from, inland and coastal.

calendar_events.title: Community Restoration Day
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us in restoring San Diego's vital wetland habitats! Get hands-on experience in restoring coastal sagescrub habitats by helping remove invasive species, planting native plants, and supporting local wildlife. Each event wraps up with a guided nature walk where you can spot native birds and learn more about the area’s natural ecosystem. What to Expect: Invasive Species Removal : Help clear invasive plants that threaten local biodiversity.
calendar_events.location: San Diego Bay Refuge, Imperial Beach, CA

calendar_events.title: North County Nature Paddle Eco Tour
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on one of our North County Kayaking adventures! Discover the natural beauty of Agua Hedionda Lagoon on a guided kayaking tour through this unique coastal ecosystem in Carlsbad. As you paddle through calm waters near the lagoon’s wetlands, keep an eye out for snowy egrets, great egrets, and great blue herons foraging along the shoreline.
calendar_events.location: California Watersports at Agua Hedionda Lagoon, Carlsbad, CA

calendar_events.title: Sacred Melodies: A Shabbat Farm Gathering to Sing, Learn, and Feast
calendar_events.description: Join us for a joyful, participatory gathering on the Farm to welcome in the Sabbath. Through the sacred melodies of Kabbalat Shabbat, approachable learning, and embodied participation, this gathering invites you to release the past week and rest in the present moment. As the sun sets, we’ll continue the celebration with a vegetarian/pescatarian potluck dinner.

calendar_events.title: Watershed Warriors: Casa de Oro
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the County of San Diego for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. Our staff will have check-in ready for you on-site. Be sure to wear comfortable clothes, closed-toed shoes, some form of sun protection (sunscreen or a hat), and bring a water bottle or two! Staff will be providing everything else you will need! Register below for more information and to get signed up!

calendar_events.title: Miramar Landfill Bus Tours
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: This two (2) hour bus tour will give you a peek behind-the-scenes to find out what really happens when you dispose of waste and clear up the myth of throwing something "away". This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY. As a safety precaution, long pants and close-toed shoes are REQUIRED . A signed waiver will be required at the start of the tour. This tour is not recommended for children under 12 years of age. Anyone under 18 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian.

calendar_events.title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special.

calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost and mulch! These products available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours.

This exhibit features a touch tank where you can interact with some of these residents while learning about them. *All presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of the staff.
calendar_events.location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA

calendar_events.title: Sea Turtle Presentation
calendar_events.description: Join us at Turtle Lagoon to learn about green sea turtles, even the ones you can find right here in San Diego Bay! A Living Coast educator will tell you all about sea turtles, like their diet, shell, fun facts, and more! *All animal presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.
calendar_events.location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA

calendar_events.title: Litter Free La Mesa: La Mesa
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of La Mesa for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. All supplies are provided. Click the registration button below for more information and to get signed up!

calendar_events.title: Watershed Warriors: El Cajon
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the County of San Diego for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. Our staff will have check-in ready for you on-site. Be sure to wear comfortable clothes, closed-toed shoes, some form of sun protection (sunscreen or a hat), and bring a water bottle or two! Staff will be providing everything else you will need! Click the register below for more information and to get signed up!

calendar_events.title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special.

calendar_events.title: Backyard Composting and Bokashi Basics Workshop
calendar_events.description: Join Solana Center for this FREE 2-hour workshop where you will get an introduction to using the bokashi method (the word "bokashi" is a Japanese term meaning fermented organic matter,) as well as the basics of traditional backyard composting. calendar_events.description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike.

calendar_events.title: Farm-to-Table Brunch
calendar_events.description: Savor the Harvest at May's Farm-to-Table Brunch Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm at our Farm-to-Table Brunch—an exquisite, seasonally inspired meal crafted by the talented Chef Mark Enciso of Order Fire BBQ. Enjoy an intimate day of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings.

calendar_events.title: REGENERATE! An Eco-Performance Fest
calendar_events.description: Tickets Artist Information Regenerate! An Eco-Performance Fest Regenerate! is a one-of-a-kind eco-performance festival featuring original works of theater, music, dance, and immersive ritual—set across the living landscape of our Farm. Wander between performances and experience how art can help us respond to the ecological challenges of our time with creativity, resilience, and hope.

calendar_events.title: Compost Workshop
calendar_events.description: Compost Workshops Offered every Sunday at 11:30 AM. Composting turns organic waste that would end up in a landfill into nutrient-rich soil. Lower your carbon footprint, divert waste, and enhance your garden all with one solution. Join us for a one-hour workshop to get you started and answer questions. (Included with cost of admission) On the first Sunday of the month, the compost workshop will be offered in English and Spanish.

calendar_events.title: North County Nature Paddle Eco Tour
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on one of our North County Kayaking adventures! Discover the natural beauty of Agua Hedionda Lagoon on a guided kayaking tour through this unique coastal ecosystem in Carlsbad. As you paddle through calm waters near the lagoon’s wetlands, keep an eye out for snowy egrets, great egrets, and great blue herons foraging along the shoreline.

calendar_events.title: Birding & Brews

Sip on smooth, nitro cold brew from Trident Coffee as you learn how their values of connection, community, and character align with our conservation mission. Then, head out on a guided walking tour along the Bayshore Bikeway at South San Diego Bay, equipped with binoculars and field guides. What to Expect: Coffee Tasting: Kick off the tour with a unique nitro cold brew tasting, crafted locally by Trident Coffee.

calendar_events.title: Compost and Mulch Giveaway – 5/30 at Chollas Lake, Gloria’s Mesa Park
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost and mulch! These products available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours.

calendar_events.title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special.

calendar_events.title: Community Restoration Day
calendar_events.description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us in restoring San Diego's vital wetland habitats! Get hands-on experience in restoring coastal sagescrub habitats by helping remove invasive species, planting native plants, and supporting local wildlife. Each event wraps up with a guided nature walk where you can spot native birds and learn more about the area’s natural ecosystem. What to Expect: Invasive Species Removal : Help clear invasive plants that threaten local biodiversity.
calendar_events.location: Living Coast Refuge, Chula Vista, CA

calendar_events.title: Eagle Presentation

calendar_events.title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
calendar_events.description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike.

calendar_events.title: Vermicomposting Workshop
calendar_events.description: Offered the last Sunday of every month at 11:30 AM. Vermicomposting uses worms to convert waste into fertilizer for your garden! This course will teach you how you can get started with your own worm bin and how to care for your worm friends! This class is included with admission. Chula Vista residents can receive free admission to the Living Coast on select Sundays! Click here to sign up.

calendar_events.title: Plant. Prepare. Pray: Shavuot Learning and Celebration
calendar_events.description: Shavuot, the Jewish holiday that commemorates the giving of the Torah on Mount Sinai, is also a deeply agricultural celebration. In ancient Israel, it marked the time when farmers brought bikkurim—the first fruits of their harvest—to Jerusalem as a gesture of gratitude and connection. After offering their choicest fruits and engaging in sacred reconnection, they returned home to enjoy the bounty of their labor.

calendar_events.title: Chefcitos
calendar_events.organization_type: CBOs, Family/Parent Organizations, Youth Organizing  & Advisory Groups
calendar_events.description: Our blueberries are back in season again! Join us this month for Chefcitos: blueberry oat bars & more. Chefcitos will have the opportunity to harvest fresh blueberries from our garden and craft them into some delicious creations that they can recreate at home. Register your K-6th graders today for a culinary adventure!

calendar_events.title: Lego Lab
calendar_events.description: Build with LEGO bricks! Take the weekly "Challenge" and display your design in our LEGO gallery. This activity encourages creativity, critical thinking and collaboration.

calendar_events.title: Supporter Appreciation Event
calendar_events.description: With a "latte" gratitude, we extend a warm invitation to join us for an appreciation event at Coastal Roots Farm’s brand-new coffee cart. Savor the perfect cup of java provided by Ironsmith Roasters (or tea if you prefer!), nosh on perfect Prager Brother’s pastries, and mingle with fellow Farm supporters, staff, and volunteers. It’s a celebration of collaboration and dedication: it’s thanks to you that Coastal Roots Farm’s mission to Nourish Community continues to grow.

calendar_events.title: Great Outdoor Shabbat
calendar_events.description: Coastal Roots Farm invites people of all ages and backgrounds to our free and festive Summer Shabbat series—Great Outdoor Shabbat! It's a true picnic-style Shabbat: bring a picnic dinner, drinks, and blankets or chairs. We'll provide live music, a fun craft for kids, a brief 10–15 minute tour of the Farm, playtime in Nature Play, and optional learning opportunities. As the sun sets over the fields, we’ll welcome Shabbat together in the beauty of the great outdoors.

calendar_events.description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity.

calendar_events.description: Have you wondered how you could get more involved? We are growing and need your help so we can all do more good together! We are looking for amazing volunteers to support our field prep and office work. We can verify community service hours (including court-ordered hours) for your time at these events.

calendar_events.title: Great Outdoor Shabbat
calendar_events.description: Coastal Roots Farm welcomes people of all ages and backgrounds for our free festive summer Shabbat series on the Farm! It’s a true picnic-style Shabbat. Bring a picnic dinner, a beverage, picnic blankets/chairs. We will provide music, a craft for the kids, a 10-15 minute tour of the Farm, some play time in Nature Play, a little learning for those that are interested, and welcome Shabbat with a beautiful sunset in the great outdoors. Upcoming dates: Friday, July 12th Friday, August 9th Register

calendar_events.title: Monthly Farm Tour
calendar_events.description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm. On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.

calendar_events.title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
calendar_events.description: Savor the Harvest at the June Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Harvest Kitchen—a full-service catering company based in North County. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings.

calendar_events.title: From Harvest to Table, A Cooking Class on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Join us for an evening of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Along the way, we’ll explore the Jewish values that guide what and how we grow, deepening our connection to the land and our food. Through this hands-on experience, you’ll have the chance to harvest fresh produce, learn new culinary skills, and share a nourishing meal with fellow participants.

This exhibit features a touch tank where you can interact with some of these residents while learning about them. *All presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of the staff.
calendar_events.location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA

calendar_events.title: Sea Turtle Presentation
calendar_events.description: Join us at Turtle Lagoon to learn about green sea turtles, even the ones you can find right here in San Diego Bay! A Living Coast educator will tell you all about sea turtles, like their diet, shell, fun facts, and more! *All animal presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.
calendar_events.location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA

calendar_events.title: Eagle Presentation
calendar_events.description: Join us at Eagle Mesa for a presentation about our bald & golden eagles! Learn about these two non-releasable raptors, like what they eat, why they reside at the Living Coast, fun facts, and more! *All events subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.
calendar_events.location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA

calendar_events.title: Compost Workshop
calendar_events.description: Compost Workshops Offered every Sunday at 11:30 AM. Composting turns organic waste that would end up in a landfill into nutrient-rich soil. Lower your carbon footprint, divert waste, and enhance your garden all with one solution. Join us for a one-hour workshop to get you started and answer questions. (Included with cost of admission) On the first Sunday of the month, the compost workshop will be offered in English and Spanish.

calendar_events.title: Farm-to-Table Dinner Featuring Kelston Moore
calendar_events.description: Savor the Harvest at the June Farm-to-Table Dinner featuring Chef from Haven Farm + Table in Encinitas Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by featured Chef Kelston Moore of Haven Farm and Table. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings.

calendar_events.title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
calendar_events.description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike.

calendar_events.title: REGENERATE! An Eco-Performance Fest
calendar_events.description: Join Coastal Roots Farm and JFEST for an evening of original eco-theater, dance, and music by some of California’s most exciting, forward-thinking artists. Earth-based Jewish wisdom will ground the event and serve as inspiration and jumping-off point for the artists. As we all navigate the darkness of the climate crisis and the environmental injustices of today, art can light our way and help us see, feel, and act with renewed joy and determination. That’s our hope for this Fest.

calendar_events.title: Paint & Nosh
calendar_events.description: Join us for an inspiring evening of art, community, and connection at Paint and Nosh! Set against the scenic backdrop of the Farm, this event invites you to explore your creativity while enjoying delicious refreshments. Guided by guest artist Tracy Lewin and our knowledgeable Farm Educators, you’ll create your own painting while learning about peleh—the Jewish value of wonder. Tracy Lewin is a local San Diego artist who is passionate about bringing art to the masses.

calendar_events.title: Monthly Farm Tour
calendar_events.description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity.

calendar_events.title: The Relationships of Earth Based Judaism
calendar_events.description: About the Series Each of us can grow and deepen the ways we relate to our earth. Let's explore this through earth-based Judaism! This five-part journey offers a chance to explore powerful Jewish emotional-spiritual frameworks that deepen our relationship with the natural world.

calendar_events.title: Baby Storytime
calendar_events.description: A lap-sit storytime introducing board books, songs, and fingerplays. Storytime will be followed by a playtime including shakers, sensory balls, and blocks. This program is for ages 0-18 months.

calendar_events.title: Eco-Jewish Play Fest
calendar_events.description: The stories we tell create the world we live in. This is a critical time in climate history. What stories will we tell? Join us for an evening of theatre as we cultivate a vision for a thriving and sustainable future. In partnership with JFEST, The Lipinksy Family San Diego Jewish Arts Festival, Coastal Roots Farm’s first-ever Eco-Jewish Play Fest will feature 7 nationally recognized local playwrights, 7 directors, and 20+ local actors.

calendar_events.title: Great Outdoor Shabbat
calendar_events.description: Coastal Roots Farm invites people of all ages and backgrounds to our free, family friendly, and festive Summer Shabbat series—Great Outdoor Shabbat! It's a true picnic-style Shabbat: bring a picnic dinner, drinks, and blankets or chairs. We'll provide live music, a fun craft for kids, a brief 10–15 minute tour of the Farm, playtime in Nature Play, and optional learning opportunities. As the sun sets over the fields, we’ll welcome Shabbat together in the beauty of the great outdoors.

calendar_events.title: Outdoor Mandala Making Workshop
calendar_events.description: Mandala is Sanskrit for “circle” and is most commonly associated with Hindu and Buddhist cultures. Join the Farm’s Director of Jewish Life, Rebecca Joy Fletcher for a hands-on, Jewish take on outdoor mandala-making, in commemoration of the upcoming Jewish fast day of Tisha B'Av. Make your own unique mandala using organic materials from the Farm’s Food Forest and engage in a conversation about the many ways we can love -- freely and abundantly.

calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the County of San Diego for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. Our staff will have check-in ready for you on-site. Be sure to wear comfortable clothes, closed-toed shoes, some form of sun protection (sunscreen or a hat), and bring a water bottle or two! Staff will be providing everything else you will need! Register below for more information and to get signed up!
calendar_events.location: Sweetwater Lane County Park, Spring Valley

calendar_events.title: Dance Party Storytime
calendar_events.description: Dance along with fun storytime songs! All children are welcome. Geared toward toddlers 18m - 3.

calendar_events.title: Vermicomposting Workshop
calendar_events.description: Offered the last Sunday of every month at 11:30 AM. Vermicomposting uses worms to convert waste into fertilizer for your garden! This course will teach you how you can get started with your own worm bin and how to care for your worm friends! This class is included with admission. Chula Vista residents can receive free admission to the Living Coast on select Sundays! Click here to sign up.

calendar_events.title: Compost Workshop
calendar_events.description: Compost Workshops Offered every Sunday at 11:30 AM. Composting turns organic waste that would end up in a landfill into nutrient-rich soil. Lower your carbon footprint, divert waste, and enhance your garden all with one solution. Join us for a one-hour workshop to get you started and answer questions. (Included with cost of admission) On the first Sunday of the month, the compost workshop will be offered in English and Spanish.

calendar_events.organization_type: CBOs, Family/Parent Organizations, Youth Organizing  & Advisory Groups
calendar_events.description: Join us at this month’s Chefcitos class for a garden-to-kitchen cooking experience, open to all K-6th graders! Chefcitos will work together with peers, practice kitchen skills and build confidence in the kitchen while making vegetable dumplings and one of our signature Olivewood dishes, Happy Rice.

calendar_events.title: Tishrei Ag Fest
calendar_events.description: The cycle of agriculture requires that we continually regenerate, repair, and renew in order to grow. Likewise, the Hebrew month of Tishrei, often referred to as the “month of beginning,” is the first month of the Jewish year and reminds us that such renewal is possible at the human level too: with each new year, we reflect where we have been and where we’re going so that we may begin again with intention.

calendar_events.title: Farm Film and Music Series
calendar_events.description: Join us this summer for a 5-part series featuring inspiring documentary films, local musicians, and meaningful conversations around equitable food systems, environmental justice, and caring for the planet. We Can Get There From Here: After years studying marine microplastics around the globe, Abby Barrows was ready to come home. In 2015, looking for a side-project that would keep her on the water, she bought the lease for an oyster farm in Deer Isle, Maine.

calendar_events.title: Nature Play Presents Musical Puppet Theater
calendar_events.description: Play. Dance. Learn. Join us at Nature Play on Friday, September 19 for a special morning of music, movement, and joy with guest performers Trees of Dreams. Get ready to sing, dance, and have some fun on the Farm. No pre-registration required. Event Details 📅 Friday, September 19 🕡 9 a.m.-12 p.m.

calendar_events.title: 42nd Annual Coastal Cleanup Day
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: The Ocean Conservancy and the California Coastal Commission partner with volunteer organizations, such as I Love A Clean San Diego to create the largest, international cleanup day. I Love A Clean San Diego leads the efforts for this day in San Diego County for the 41st year in a row. Data collected from this event feeds into the world’s largest database on marine debris.

calendar_events.title: Intergenerational Rosh Hashanah
calendar_events.description: Coastal Roots Farm invites you to Intergenerational Rosh Hashanah! Join us to prepare for Rosh Hashanah, the Jewish New Year, through learning, crafting, and culinary creativity! At this intergenerational gathering, we'll explore meanings of the holiday, make a New Year treat from scratch, and create a festive craft to decorate your home.

calendar_events.title: Sun-Day Celebration & Dayenu Advocacy Circle
calendar_events.description: A New Year for Action! Sun-Day Celebration & Dayenu Advocacy Circle Celebrate Sun-Day at Coastal Roots Farm—the launch of San Diego’s First Dayenu Circle Join us at Coastal Roots Farm for the launch our new Dayenu Circle, a monthly advocacy group rooted in Jewish values and hope-filled climate action. This first gathering coincides with Sun-Day, a national day of action for clean energy.

calendar_events.title: Rosh Hashanah on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Rosh Hashanah, the Jewish new year, is a fall holiday that celebrates the completion of another year and serves as a time of rejoice and introspection. Gather with us to celebrate the new year, reflect together, and prepare for what is to come. Geared towards young families, especially good for ages 2-10.

calendar_events.description: Experience Film, Music, and Meaningful Conversation on the Farm Join us on the Farm for an evening of community, live music, and transformative storytelling. calendar_events.title: Farm Film & Music Series: Tribal Waters
calendar_events.description: Join us this summer for a 5-part series featuring inspiring documentary films, local musicians, and meaningful conversations around equitable food systems, environmental justice, and caring for the planet. Thursday, September 28th - Tribal Waters directed by Jon Klaczkiewicz, a film by Teton Gravity Research presented by Patagonia, documents the course and history of the magnificent Wind River, now the home waters of the Eastern Shoshone and Northern Arapaho Tribes.

calendar_events.title: Harvest to Table Cooking Class
calendar_events.description: Join us for an evening of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Together, we’ll prepare a Farm-Fresh menu using ingredients harvested straight from the Farm. Along the way, we’ll explore the Jewish values that guide what and how we grow, deepening our connection to the land and our food. Through this hands-on experience, you’ll have the chance to harvest fresh produce, learn new culinary skills, and share a nourishing meal with fellow participants.

calendar_events.title: Nature Play Rosh Hashanah
calendar_events.description: Come celebrate a sweet Jewish New Year with us at Nature Play! Join us for a fun-filled morning on the Farm, complete with Rosh Hashanah-themed arts and crafts, story time, songs, and more delightful surprises! $8 per child and $8 for the first adult. Click to learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.

calendar_events.title: Queer in the Sukkah
calendar_events.description: Thursday, October 5 @ 6:00 pm to 8:00 pm - Preregistration is required. No walk-ups will be allowed. You are invited to Coastal Roots Farm’s first-ever queer gathering and party in celebration of Sukkot, the Jewish Harvest Festival sometimes referred to as "the time of our joy." Soak in the magic of our farm; experience joyful Sukkot traditions; get inspired by Hanz Enyeart, Queer Jewish scholar and writer (check out his column in The Rage magazine!) who will invite in the stories of...

calendar_events.title: San Diego Climate Week: Threads Between Us Film Premiere & Sustainable Business Mixer
calendar_events.description: Coastal Roots Farm is hosting a San Diego Climate Week Sustainable Business Mixer! Join San Diego Climate Week, in partnership with thinkPARALLAX, for an unforgettable evening at Coastal Roots Farm bringing together local businesses, climate activists, and community members to foster connections and inspire action. We're screening the premiere of The Threads Between Us.

calendar_events.title: Sukkot Harvest Festival 2022
calendar_events.description: Join us for the 10th Annual Sukkot Harvest Festival at Coastal Roots Farm! The Sukkot Harvest Festival is the Farm's biggest event of the year, inspired by the Jewish tradition of gathering community outdoors to celebrate the end of an abundant summer harvest and welcome in the fall season and all it brings. Meet us under the sukkah (temporary shelter) for a day filled with live music, food, and activities for all ages.

calendar_events.title: Shabbat Potluck: Celebrating Sukkot
calendar_events.description: Sukkot Shabbat Dinner Join Director of Jewish Life Rebecca Joy Fletcher for a celebration of community, soil, and soul in this Shabbat and Sukkot dinner celebration inside the Farm sukkah! Connect with friends new and old, share stories of abundance and harvest, and engage in Sukkot learning and ritual which bind us more closely to love of the earth and its bounty. There will be opportunity to meet, laugh, and sing! No need to identify as Jewish to thoroughly enjoy.

calendar_events.title: Litter Free La Mesa: La Mesa
calendar_events.organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
calendar_events.description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of La Mesa for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. All supplies are provided. Click the registration button below for more information and to get signed up!

calendar_events.title: Sukkot Harvest Festival 2025
calendar_events.description: Festival Activities Sponsors Tickets When it comes to celebrating the earth, ancient Jewish tradition recommends we do it together. Coastal Roots Farm invites you to celebrate with us at our 13th annual Sukkot Harvest Festival, our biggest event of the year! This intergenerational event features live music, local food, hands-on workshops, and engaging activities for all ages as we celebrate the bounty of our Farm and the spirit of our diverse community.

calendar_events.title: Pride in the Sukkah
calendar_events.description: LGBTQ+ Sukkot Celebration at Coastal Roots Farm Come celebrate the beauty of LGBTQ+ community and the harvest holiday of Sukkot under Coastal Roots Farm’s big sukkah! Together we’ll gather with friends old and new, share festive food and drink, and explore the meaning of Sukkot through Earth Based Judaism learning. Sukkot is one of the most communal Jewish holidays. In the sukkah, a traditional temporary outdoor structure, we leave the walls open so that everyone feels welcome.

calendar_events.title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & Psalms
calendar_events.description: Qigong & Psalms Join Coastal Roots Farm and guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman, Director of Spiritual Life at Jewish Family Service, for an embodied exploration of Qigong & Psalms. In this four-part, interactive series, we will draw on qigong to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies while connecting more deeply to the imagery and inspiration found in the ancient Book of Psalms.

calendar_events.title: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle
calendar_events.description: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle Join us at Coastal Roots Farm for our new Dayenu Circle, a monthly advocacy group rooted in Jewish values and hope-filled climate action. What is Dayenu?Dayenu is a national Jewish movement responding to the climate crisis with hope, action, and community. Local Dayenu Circles bring people together to learn, connect, and take meaningful steps toward a more just and sustainable future. You don’t need to identify as Jewish to participate—everyone is welcome.

calendar_events.title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
calendar_events.description: Savor the Harvest at the October Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Chef Alex Velasco. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings. With a 60-guest maximum, this experience ensures a cozy and communal atmosphere.

calendar_events.title: The Big Birthday Block Party
calendar_events.description: Enjoy free admission to the Museum, explore all exhibits, a 21+ rooftop bar, enjoy food, drink, music, deals, and more.

calendar_events.title: Sukkot Harvest Festival 2024
calendar_events.description: When it comes to celebrating the earth, ancient Jewish tradition recommends we do it together. Coastal Roots Farm invites you to celebrate with us at our 12th annual Sukkot Harvest Festival, our biggest event of the year! This intergenerational event features live music, local food, hands-on workshops, and engaging activities for all ages as we celebrate the bounty of our Farm and the spirit of our diverse community.

calendar_events.title: SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering
calendar_events.description: The SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering at Coastal Roots Farm is an opportunity for Tribal members in Southern California that are actively cultivating or interested in joining the food sovereignty movement among Indigenous Nations. Examples of people active in food sovereignty work include Tribal farmers, seed keepers, land stewards, hunters, livestock owners, foragers, botanists, fishers, medicine makers, and cooks.

calendar_events.description: A new adventure club for kids ages 8-14. Come roll a D20 and immerse yourself in the world of Dungeons & Dragons!

calendar_events.title: Coastal Roots Farm-to-Table Dinner
calendar_events.description: Dine with us during an intimate Farm-to-Table Dinner at Coastal Roots Farm! Featuring Chef Francesco Bonsi Sicilian-born, locally-based personal chef and food educator rooted in supporting local farms and giving guests a true taste of Italy in each meal that he creates. Including seasonal, organic farm-fresh produce. Supporting the Farm's impact in the community.

calendar_events.title: Intro to Earth-Based Judaism Series (4 Classes)
calendar_events.description: In this four-part pilot class, we will gather in lovely locations across the Farm to explore 4 basic pathways into the spiritual revolution called Earth-Based Judaism -- and how to bring those pathways back into your life. The goal is more resilience, earth-love, responsibility, and joy. Our learning will include sacred Jewish texts and wisdom, hearty conversation, on-our-feet exploration and play, and moments of quiet reflection. We'll also provide coffee, pastries, and time to schmooze.

calendar_events.title: Havdalah on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Join us in celebrating Havdalah on the Farm and mark the end of Shabbat! Spend time in the great outdoors and gather in community to usher in the new week. Activities include: making a farm-fresh besamim (spice bag) and tasting local wines. Plus! A chance to make your own locally-sourced beeswax candle. Havdalah ceremony will be led by Camp Mountain Chai's musical specialist, Marshall Voit followed by dancing and music. Family friendly. Pre-registration encouraged. Register now

calendar_events.title: Paint & Nosh
calendar_events.description: Join us for an inspiring evening of art, community, and connection at Paint and Nosh! Set against the scenic backdrop of the Farm, this event invites you to explore your creativity while enjoying delicious refreshments. Guided by guest artist Suchita and our knowledgeable Farm Educators, you’ll create your own painting while learning about peleh—the Jewish value of wonder.

calendar_events.title: Play-to-Learn
calendar_events.description: Join us on Thursdays where your child can explore independent learning stations and participate in activities that promote socialization, motor skills, and more through play. Geared to children 0-5.

calendar_events.title: Natural Dye Workshop for Teens
calendar_events.description: Natural dyes can be made from materials such as leaves, bark, flowers, fruit, minerals, and more! Creating dyes from natural materials cuts out the use of harsh chemicals that can be harmful to people and the planet. Learn how to make natural dyes using materials found at home or in your yard to tie dye a bandana to take home! Led by Aliza Zollman, a passionate student who has been moved by the environmental impacts of the textile industry and fast fashion. For teens ages 11-18 only.

calendar_events.title: Compost Workshop
calendar_events.description: Compost Workshops Offered every Sunday at 11:30 AM. Composting turns organic waste that would end up in a landfill into nutrient-rich soil. Lower your carbon footprint, divert waste, and enhance your garden all with one solution. Join us for a one-hour workshop to get you started and answer questions. (Included with cost of admission) On the first Sunday of the month, the compost workshop will be offered in English and Spanish.

calendar_events.title: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle
calendar_events.description: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle Join us at Coastal Roots Farm for our new Dayenu Circle, a monthly advocacy group rooted in Jewish values and hope-filled climate action. What is Dayenu?Dayenu is a national Jewish movement responding to the climate crisis with hope, action, and community. Local Dayenu Circles bring people together to learn, connect, and take meaningful steps toward a more just and sustainable future. You don’t need to identify as Jewish to participate—everyone is welcome.

calendar_events.title: Fun with Friends
calendar_events.description: Join us for fun unstructured activities every second and fourth Wednesday of the month from 10:30 a.m.-12:30 p.m. for adults with disabilities and their caregivers.Rotating activities can include puzzles, coloring sheets, word searches, basic crafts, board games, and sensory items! No registration required. Program starts Wednesday, February 18, 2026.The Fun With Friends program will be held in the Teen Room on May 6, May 20, and June 3, 2026, due to Community Room unavailability.

calendar_events.title: Jingle Jets 2025
calendar_events.description: Where a million lights create magical nights! The USS Midway Museum’s holiday tradition returns for its 3rd year, brighter, bigger, and more magical than ever! Visit on select nights from November 28 to December 30 to enjoy over a million dazzling lights, festive performances, and holiday cheer for the entire family.

calendar_events.title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
calendar_events.description: Savor the Harvest at the December Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Chef Chuck Samuelson of Heal the Earth. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings.

calendar_events.title: Harvest to Table Cooking Class
calendar_events.description: Join us for an evening of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Together, we’ll prepare a Farm-Fresh menu using ingredients harvested straight from the Farm. Along the way, we’ll explore the Jewish values that guide what and how we grow, deepening our connection to the land and our food. Through this hands-on experience, you’ll have the chance to harvest fresh produce, learn new culinary skills, and share a nourishing meal with fellow participants.

calendar_events.title: Monthly Farm Tour
calendar_events.description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity.

calendar_events.title: Fall Garden Workshop Series
calendar_events.description: Our Fall Workshops' theme is Soil: Join us at the Beginning. Every gardening journey starts with soil, and we will be uncovering the mysteries of soil. Learn with Christina Cranwell, our experienced Outdoor Educator who has been farming 5+ years across multiple states, and Nora Mitchell, our Outdoor Educator with 30+ years of teaching and hands-on organic farming experience.

calendar_events.title: Chanukah on the Farm
calendar_events.description: Kick off your Chanukah celebration at Coastal Roots Farm! Join us for activities on the Farm including: candle and dreidel making out of natural items, harvest and taste a Farm-fresh snack, learn about photosynthesis and how to be a steward of the land. Plus! Make herb infused oil using Farm-fresh herbs. Perfect for young families with little ones under the age of 8.

calendar_events.title: Fiddler Sing-Along
calendar_events.description: In honor of the first night of Chanukah, join us for a sing-along celebration of the beloved film Fiddler on the Roof! In partnership with our neighbors at The Hive, we are excited to announce an evening on the Farm filled with tasty snacks, warm drinks, dress up, and a whole lot of singing. Enjoy root veggie latkes (with Farm veggies) made fresh by The Hive's Chef Tiffani, along with apple cider to keep you warm. There will be ample heat lamp and blankets.

calendar_events.title: Fields of Light: A Chanukah Celebration
calendar_events.description: Join Coastal Roots Farm for an intergenerational celebration of Chanukah! On the final night of Chanukah, when we uplift all eight candles and with them boundless light, we come together to honor the holiday's spirit of resilience and hope. 🗓️ Sunday, December 21 🕔 4-6 p.m. 🎫 $25/adults, $10/child This event is expected to sell out! Pre-registration required. Unfortunately, walk-ups will not be accepted. During this time of heightened awareness, please expect additional security.

This course will teach you how you can get started with your own worm bin and how to care for your worm friends! This class is included with admission. Chula Vista residents can receive free admission to the Living Coast on select Sundays! Click here to sign up.

calendar_events.title: Compost Workshop
calendar_events.description: Compost Workshops Offered every Sunday at 11:30 AM. Composting turns organic waste that would end up in a landfill into nutrient-rich soil. Lower your carbon footprint, divert waste, and enhance your garden all with one solution. Join us for a one-hour workshop to get you started and answer questions. (Included with cost of admission) On the first Sunday of the month, the compost workshop will be offered in English and Spanish.

program_listings.title: ALL FRIENDS NATURE SCHOOL
program_listings.description: All Friends Nature School is a place and play based nature school. We are full nature immersion. No day is ever the same. Each child brings a backpack with snack, lunch, water bottle, and a change of clothes. We do not teach children what to think. We want them to have their own experiences and deve

program_listings.title: AEF Little Learners
program_listings.description: Learn & Play Little Learners Educational FUN AEF Little Learners AEF Little Learners is a fun interactive story hour for toddlers and preschoolers held throughout the year and consists of age-appropriate picture books, songs, and includes playtime and crafts. Children also get a soft introduction into our “Wheel of Experts” which is a multiple pathway program that brings STEAM based learning to the classroom.

program_listings.title: Camps
program_listings.description: CALENDAR ReadyBreak Camps ELEMENTARY SCHOOL CAMPS Ready Break Camps 2026 This year, we are excited to introduce a new format for our Elementary School Summer Camps! Instead of selecting a single subject for the week, campers will register by incoming grade level and rotate through a variety of fun activities throughout the week. Each day, students will experience a different subject area such as art, cooking, STEM, PE, games, and more.

program_listings.title: Camps FAQ
program_listings.description: Information Frequently Asked Questions Got A Question? Get your answers here. I have registered and paid, now what? You will receive a confirmation email with your registration selection, once it has been processed. Parents/Guardians will also receive a welcome letter from their student’s instructor approx. one week prior to their camp assignment with information specific to the camp. Will meals be provided for my child? No meal service will be provided.

program_listings.title: Handbook & Waivers
program_listings.description: Keep safe Camp Waivers Click the buttons below to read, download and print the required forms for your child to attend the ReadyBreak camps. These forms need to be completed and presented on the first day of camp. AUSD READYBREAK SUMMER CAMPS PARENT HANDBOOK Download AUTHORIZATION & WAIVER FORM Download PHOTOGRAPHIC and/or VIDEO RELEASE FORM Download Years Established Students

program_listings.title: Punkin' Chunkin'
program_listings.description: Learn & Play Punkin’ Chunkin’ Educational FUN What is Punkin’ Chunkin’? Using their knowledge of history, math, and physics, students build trebuchets, a medieval siege engine, to launch pumpkins and golf balls, competing for distance and accuracy. They practice teamwork while learning wood handling and the use of hand tools. It is also a popular activity around the world.

program_listings.title: ReadyBreak Camps
program_listings.description: Learn & Play Camps Educational FUN ReadyBreak Camps ReadyBreak Camps returns every year with a stunning growth in attendance over the previous year. Hundreds of students participated in camps of their choice. Some of the popular previous camp themes were: Art, Music & Theater Personal Health & Development Science, Math & STEM LEGO Labs Animal Husbandry Cuisine & Gardening Sports Book Camps 2025 Camp Gallery

program_listings.title: Wheel Of Experts
program_listings.description: Learn & Play Wheel of Experts Educational FUN STEAM Based Learning “Wheel of Experts” is a multiple pathway program that brings STEAM based learning to the classroom. Each week, students from Kindergarten-8th grade have the opportunity to participate in hands on enrichment topics such as Coding/Robotics, Science Labs, Music, and Gardening/Aquaponics – creating a direct pathway to electives offered in 6th to 8th grade. Instruction is to Next Generation Science Standards.

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego Desert State Park BioBlitz 2026
program_listings.description: Discover. Document. Protect the Desert. Join scientists and fellow nature lovers for an exciting, hands-on weekend exploring the incredible biodiversity of Anza-Borrego Desert State Park. This collaborative BioBlitz brings together the community and experts to survey, document, and celebrate the living organisms that make this desert so extraordinary.

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego Night Hike
program_listings.description: Join us for a peaceful moonlit hike through the desert, where wide-open skies, rugged terrain, and the quiet beauty of the landscape come alive after dark.

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego Plant Walk
program_listings.description: Discover the secrets of the desert’s remarkable plant life

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego Under the Stars: Culp Valley
program_listings.description: Experience the night sky like never before in historic Culp Valley, nestled within Anza-Borrego Desert State Park—an official International Dark Sky Park. Explore stars, planets, and constellations through high-powered telescopes, guided by an expert astronomer.

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego Watercolor Workshop
program_listings.description: Connect with the desert and your creativity! Unleash your inner artist in this hands-on watercolor painting workshop led by local artist Shirley Spencer. Whether you're a beginner or experienced painter, you'll learn how to beautifully capture Anza-Borrego’s unique landscape.

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego on Two Wheels: Guided Biking Tour
program_listings.description: Come discover the Park on two wheels! Join ABF for the reintroduction of its beloved guided biking tours. This moderate tour will take us to the beautiful Blair Valley in Anza-Borrego Desert State Park. Participants will ride approximately 11 miles on jeep roads with 800 feet of elevation change to the base of Ghost Mountain, Morteros Trailhead, Pictographs Trailhead, and little Blair Valley. Participants should bring their own mountain or fat tire bikes, plenty of water, snacks, and sun protect

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego under the Stars: Clark Dry Lake
program_listings.description: Discover the magic of the universe above one of California’s premier Dark Sky Communities!

program_listings.title: Anza-Borrego under the Stars: Coyote Canyon
program_listings.description: Join Us for a Stargazing Adventure in Anza-Borrego! Experience the night sky like never before in beautiful Coyote Canyon, nestled within Anza-Borrego Desert State Park—an official International Dark Sky Park. Explore stars, planets, and constellations through high-powered telescopes, guided by an expert astronomer.

program_listings.title: Astronomy Workshop: “The Christmas Star”
program_listings.description: Travel through time and space as we explore the legend of the "Christmas Star"—a celestial mystery that has fascinated people for over two millennia. Dive into the rich blend of science, history, and mythology to uncover what may have lit the skies over 2,000 years ago.

program_listings.title: Astrophotography Workshop: The Art of Star Trail Photography
program_listings.description: Learn the art of long exposures under Borrego’s dark skies

program_listings.title: BIPOC Environmentalist Campout
program_listings.description: Join us for the 1st SoCal BIPOC Environmentalist Campout where we will center connection and joy in the environmental field! Take part in a family-friendly weekend of community, connection, and conservation at Anza Borrego Desert State Park. This event is a unique opportunity for Black, Indigenous, and POC to come together in nature, share stories, and learn from each other. Whether you're a seasoned environmentalist or just starting your journey, this campout is for you to build your network as

program_listings.title: Certified California Naturalist Course
program_listings.description: Sign up to become a certified California Naturalist with Anza-Borrego Foundation through the lens of the desert! We’ve teamed up with University of California Environmental Stewards to offer an in-depth course for adult learners in the natural processes that make California home to more plant and animal species than any other state in the United […]

program_listings.title: Chill Hike
program_listings.description: “Chill Hikes” is our NEW beginner’s hiking program that is FREE for members! Our first Chill Hike will take place at Borrego Palm Canyon Nature Trail, a loop trail that’s the most popular hike in Anza-Borrego Desert State Park!

program_listings.title: December Family Camp
program_listings.description: et ready for an unforgettable family adventure in the great outdoors! Anza-Borrego Family Camping is a 3-day, 2-night escape to Anza-Borrego Desert State Park. And the best part? We take care of everything—camping gear, delicious meals, and tons of fun activities are all included!

program_listings.title: December Member Hike- Yaqui Well Nature Trail
program_listings.description: Join us for our December Member Hike, Yaqui Well Nature Trail! This trail is one of the self-guided nature trails in the Park. We will pick up a trail guide at the trailhead and follow the numbered markers along the way to learn about the history and botany of this area in the Park. If you […]

program_listings.title: Desert Connections Mixer at Epicurious Social Club
program_listings.description: Join us for a fun-filled and informational Happy Hour! Meet Anza–Borrego Foundation staff and learn how we support Anza-Borrego Desert State Park as the official Park Partner.

program_listings.title: Feathered Friends of the Anza-Borrego Desert Region (In-Person Seminar)
program_listings.description: Calling all avian enthusiasts! Do you ever wonder which birds make Anza-Borrego Desert State Park their year-round home? Or which ones stop in the region to rest during their long cross-country routes? This lecture will highlight a few of the wonderful resident and seasonal birds that call the State Park home.

program_listings.title: Find That Lizard: An Anza-Borrego Artventure
program_listings.description: Join Dr. Earyn McGee, creator of #FindThatLizard, along with Outdoor Afro and EnvironmentallyU for a fun nature and art adventure in the desert!

program_listings.title: From Species to Systems: The Relationships of Desert Organisms
program_listings.description: Join us for an evening lecture and Q&A with Dr. Marie Simovich, professor, naturalist, and co-author of the iconic A Natural History of the Anza-Borrego Region. Marie (“Ree”) will reveal the secrets of Community Ecology in the desert – the study of how communities of organisms interact with and are affected by one another.

program_listings.title: From the Mountains to the Sea: A Three-Day Birding Adventure
program_listings.description: Explore Southern California’s rich birdlife on a guided journey from the mountains to the Salton Sea to the desert. Join expert birders Gjon Hazard and Thomas Myers for an unforgettable birding experience as we explore and learn about the rich diversity of inland southern California (130+ species possible).

program_listings.description: Geology of Anza-Borrego and Earthquakes Around the World- virtual & in-person lecture.

program_listings.title: Guided Biking Tour: Thimble Trail
program_listings.description: Come discover the Park on two wheels! Join us for an intermediate guided biking tour in Anza-Borrego Desert State Park!

program_listings.title: Guided Driving Tour: 78 Washes
program_listings.description: If you're looking for a fun and interesting drive to some of our desert's most beautiful spots, then this excursion is for you. We'll explore the desert by wheel and foot, to show you stunning canyons, geologic wonders, hidden palm oases, winding washes, and a sweeping overlook.

program_listings.title: Guided Driving Tour: Canyon Sin Nombre
program_listings.description: A full-day desert adventure through slot canyons, badlands, and mud caves Discover the rugged beauty of the lesser-traveled southern region of Anza-Borrego Desert State Park on this all-day, intermediate-level off-road adventure. Experience slot canyons, sweeping desert vistas, and unique geological features like mud caves.

program_listings.title: Intermediate Four-Wheel Driving Tour
program_listings.description: Join Anza-Borrego Foundation for our season’s first intermediate four-wheel drive adventure through Anza-Borrego Desert State Park! Participants will venture along sandy trails and washes to reach spectacular stops such as Hawk Canyon, Badlands, Pumpkin Patch, and palm oases. Participants can expect areas with deep or soft sand, rocks to drive over or around, and some uneven terrain. Please note that 4WD vehicles are required. Participants should bring adequate water and snacks/food for the tou

program_listings.title: Intermediate Guided Driving Tour: Pinyon Mountain and Blair Valley
program_listings.description: Join us for a 24-mile adventure featuring rugged desert terrain, scenic climbs, and nearly 3,000 feet of elevation gain. This intermediate-level drive explores some of the region’s most scenic backcountry routes.

program_listings.title: Intermediate Guided Driving Tour: Yaqui Well
program_listings.description: Join us for a 24-mile adventure featuring rugged desert terrain, scenic climbs, and nearly 3,000 feet of elevation gain. This intermediate-level drive explores some of the region’s most scenic backcountry routes.

program_listings.title: Intermediate Hike
program_listings.description: Join ABF for our first intermediate hike of the season! This scenic and moderately challenging six-mile hike will gain approximately 1000 ft of elevation and take you through some of the most fascinating landscapes in ABDSP. Participants will hike The Slot, a narrow and winding sandstone canyon known for its dramatic, towering walls and tight passages that make for a thrilling adventure.

program_listings.title: Interpretive Hike: A Cahuilla History of Coyote Canyon
program_listings.description: Join us for a guided walk through Coyote Canyon where we’ll explore the cultural significance of the area from a Cahuilla perspective, identify plants with historical and medicinal uses, and take in stunning views at Santa Catarina Vista.

program_listings.title: Interpretive Hike: Anza-Borrego’s Active Faults
program_listings.description: Join us for a hands-on exploration of the Coyote Creek strand of the San Jacinto Fault, where the landscape itself reveals the movement of Earth’s crust. This guided field trip highlights classic examples of fault geomorphology and offers an up-close look at the evidence scientists use to map and interpret active faults.

program_listings.title: January Member Hike- Hornblend Canyon
program_listings.description: Join us for a hike in Hornblende Canyon led by Board Member & retired District Superintendent of the Colorado Desert District, Dave Van Cleve! The canyon is named after the dominant rock found here, hornblende-biotite tonalite, an intrusive igneous rock consisting of feldspar, quartz, mica and/or amphibole minerals (like hornblende). We will hike to a […]

program_listings.description: Join us for a hike to Mountain Palm Springs Trail, where we will hike to a palm oasis. The highlight is the Mountain Palm Springs, an oasis with California fan palms, providing a stark contrast […]

program_listings.title: Moderate Hike: Inner Pastures View via Moonlight Canyon
program_listings.description: Discover Hidden Vistas in the southern end of the Park! Join us for a scenic, moderate-strenuous hike through Moonlight Canyon to a stunning wilderness overlook—and cap it off with a relaxing soak in natural hot springs.

program_listings.title: Mountain, Sea and Desert Birding Tour
program_listings.description: Join us for the return of our Mountain, Sea and Desert Birding Tour! Two expert birders, Gjon Hazard and Thomas Myers, will lead the group on a three-day birding class from the mountains to the (Salton) sea to the desert. Gjon and Thomas have scouted the best birding sites of the region and are eager to help you get your best bird sightings of the year. They’ll also discuss the ecology, conservation, and taxonomy of the birds that we’ll see.

program_listings.organization: Birch Aquarium at Scripps Institution of Oceanography

program_listings.title: Summer Camp FAQs
program_listings.organization: Birch Aquarium at Scripps Institution of Oceanography
program_listings.description: Summer Camp PoliciesCamper Age PolicyEnroll the camper according to their age by the start of camp. Our programs are designed for the specified age groupings, and we want to ensure the camper has the best experience possible. Should a camper be registered for the wrong age grouping, we reserve the right to cancel your registration or provide you with an alternate camp, if available.
