program_listings.title: Animal Helpers
program_listings.description: Animal Behavior and Training Workshop The NMMF has partnered with the Girl Scouts of San Diego to teach local girls all about how animals help people and people help animals, with an emphasis on the special relationship that we have with dolphins, sea lions, and other marine mammals. We touch on marine mammal behavior and basic methods for training any type of animal.

program_listings.title: Dolphin Doctor Workshop
program_listings.description: In this workshop, students are guided through an interactive mock dolphin health assessment, explore the field of marine mammal bioacoustics, and learn how scientists can use sound to help care for marine mammals. From conducting health assessments with trained dolphins to working alongside wild pods, our team is dedicated to marine mammal care.

program_listings.title: Marine Mammal Conservation Workshop
program_listings.description: The NMMF is a world leader in marine mammal conservation efforts, with programs helping endangered and threatened species such as bottlenose dolphins in the Gulf of Mexico, California Sea Lions, the critically endangered Vaquita porpoise, and the Southern Resident Killer Whales. This program teaches basic marine mammal biology and ecology and highlights the exciting research of the NMMF.

program_listings.title: NMMF Scientist Snapshot: Brian Balmer
program_listings.description: NMMF Scientist Snapshot: Brian Balmer Test Test Test

program_listings.title: Pop for a Porpoise
program_listings.description: POP for a Porpoise Workshop Prevent Ocean Plastics In this workshop, students will focus on ways that plastic pollution affects marine animals, such as entanglement and ingestion of plastics by dolphins, whales, sea turtles, sea birds, and fish. We specifically highlight our efforts to save the Vaquita porpoise, the most endangered marine mammal in the world (www.VaquitaCPR.org).

program_listings.title: Under Pressure Workshop
program_listings.description: Discovering the Incredible Adaptations that Enable Marine Mammals to Explore the Ocean’s Depths Marine Mammals share many traits with humans – they have large brains for their body size, they breathe air, and can become infected by similar diseases. However, one unique difference is that marine mammals are capable of deep diving by tolerating immense pressure. This workshop will focus on marine mammal adaptations to diving in the ocean and how they compare to human diving physiology.

program_listings.title: Voices in the Sea
program_listings.description: Voices in the Sea Workshop Marine mammals rely on sound for communication, foraging, navigation, and predator avoidance. The NMMF explores the sounds these animals make, the sounds they hear, and the effects of human-made noise on their behavior and physiology. The NMMF has developed advanced instrumentation to test the hearing of wild marine mammals and determine safe sound exposure levels.

program_listings.title: Workshops
program_listings.description: Our Ocean-Based STEAM Workshops highlight the NMMF’s discoveries about marine mammals to promote creative thinking and inspire a greater commitment to the conservation of marine mammals and their habitats. Our program offers a unique opportunity for you to: Connect with Marine Mammal Experts: Our Community Engagement staff and volunteers include marine mammal scientists, veterinarians, conservationists, and care experts. Learn from their experiences, insights, and ongoing field work.

program_listings.title: You Can Be a STEM Professional
program_listings.description: You Can Be a STEM Professional Workshop Inspiring the Next Generation Participants will hear from STEM (science, technology, engineering, and mathematics) professionals in a wide variety of fields, including marine mammal science and veterinary medicine, while participating in hands-on STEM demonstrations.

program_listings.title: NSBE Programs
program_listings.organization: National Society of Black Engineers - San Diego
program_listings.description: From STEM summer camps and collegiate leadership to professional certifications, NSBE programs build the skills engineers need to excel at every stage of their career.

program_listings.title: NSBE SEEK
program_listings.organization: National Society of Black Engineers - San Diego
program_listings.description: NSBE SEEK is a free, three-week summer program offering hands-on STEM experiences for students in grades 3–5. Ignite a passion for engineering early.

program_listings.title: T.O.R.C.H.
program_listings.organization: National Society of Black Engineers - San Diego
program_listings.description: T.O.R.C.H. empowers NSBE collegiate members to lead community outreach initiatives that connect engineering to underserved communities and inspire the next generation of STEM students.

program_listings.title: Ten80
program_listings.organization: National Society of Black Engineers - San Diego
program_listings.description: Ten80 through NSBE engages students in grades 6–12 with STEM challenges tied to real-world engineering and entrepreneurship, using racing and design as the hook.

program_listings.title: Try-Math-A-Lon
program_listings.organization: National Society of Black Engineers - San Diego
program_listings.description: Try-Math-A-Lon is NSBE’s math enrichment program for early learners, building numerical fluency and enthusiasm for mathematics before the transition to middle school.

program_listings.organization: National Society of Black Engineers - San Diego
program_listings.description: NSBE’s VEX IQ Challenge gives students in grades 3–8 the chance to design, build, and program robots — developing teamwork, engineering thinking, and problem-solving skills.

program_listings.organization: National Society of Black Engineers - San Diego

program_listings.title: San Elijo Lagoon

program_listings.title: Community Programs
program_listings.description: Community Programs engage young people, their families, and neighbors within the school-shed to ensure everyone sees science and conservation as relevant to their lives. Watershed Avengers Watershed Avengers is the cornerstone of these programs. It is designed to use ocean science, watershed education, and local resources such as canyons, as a platform to teach science and engage participants in environmental stewardship actions.

program_listings.title: Leader Programs
program_listings.description: Our Leaders Programs engage young people along a pathway to college and career to ensure students who want to become science and conservation leaders are provided the tools to get there. These programs engage over 100 middle school, high school, and college-age young people per year in a series of interconnected after-school and summer programs and support services.

program_listings.title: Student Programs
program_listings.description: Student Programs engage students and their teachers within their instructional settings to ensure every student is engaged in hands-on science. Ocean Science Explorers The cornerstone of our student programs, Ocean Science Explorers provides early, continued exposure to science by facilitating science learning in the classroom and field and professional development and resources for classroom teachers.

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: 1,000 Days
program_listings.description: At this beachside workshop, you’ll set up an outdoor studio to document the impact of pollution and restricted access to public spaces. Through photography and sign-making, you’ll bring awareness to the effects of contamination from the Tijuana River and discuss the importance of land stewardship. Standing on a beach that has been closed for over […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Art In Action
program_listings.description: In this kickoff session, you’ll get to know the Land + Lenses program, meet the facilitators, and explore the park through a hands-on scavenger hunt. Using cameras or drawing materials, you’ll document native plants and identify misplaced objects while engaging in a community clean-up. You’ll also have the chance to turn collected trash into creative […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Compost & Connect
program_listings.description: Explore composting as a metaphor for transformation and healing. Guided by a guest artist, you’ll create compost dolls—biodegradable figures that can be planted or turned into chia pets. Through hands-on activities and discussion, you’ll reflect on cycles of life, grief, and renewal, considering how nature models regeneration through decay and growth. Come to a free […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Final Exhibition!
program_listings.description: 📅 Saturday, May 31, 2025🕐 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM📍 The Front Arte y CulturaFree event | All ages welcome | Food provided Join us for the culminating exhibition of Land + Lenses, a community arts program that invited youth and families to explore the rich landscapes of Otay Valley Regional Park and YMCA Camp […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Landscape Dreams
program_listings.description: Using a Polaroid camera, you’ll capture landscapes and overlay your images with personal reflections, drawings, or written intentions. Through guided prompts, you’ll explore your relationship with the land and dream about what an ideal park or community space could look like. At the end, you’ll contribute to a collective collage, creating a visual representation of […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Nature in Focus
program_listings.description: Discover how portraiture and nature photography can be powerful tools for environmental advocacy. You’ll learn photography techniques using DSLR cameras and experiment with capturing landscapes and portraits. Through discussion, you’ll reflect on your connection to nature and explore how images can be used to inspire change and bring awareness to environmental issues. Come to a […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Park Not Dump
program_listings.description: You’ll turn your focus to the park’s environment, exploring how contamination affects interconnected landscapes. You’ll discuss the relationship between land, water, and pollution while using photography and sign-making to advocate for environmental responsibility. This session encourages you to reclaim and reimagine public spaces through creative expression and activism. Come to a free Land and Lenses […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Planting Memories
program_listings.description: In this hands-on workshop, you’ll create botanical prints on fabric or paper using native plants and cyanotype techniques. You’ll learn about photography’s connection to botanical documentation while reflecting on personal and cultural memories tied to the land. As you experiment with eco-printing, you’ll explore how nature can be both an artistic medium and a storyteller. […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Portraits of Belonging
program_listings.description: Step into a rotating outdoor photo studio where you’ll create portraits inspired by the four elements—earth, water, air, and fire. Each element represents themes like grounding, movement, and transformation. You’ll use props, explore different photography techniques, and reflect on your relationship to nature and belonging. By the end, you’ll have a series of portraits that […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Rituals & Remedies
program_listings.description: Engage your senses in an immersive exploration of herbalism and ritual. You’ll move through interactive stations, tasting locally foraged teas, crafting intention-based spell jars, making smudge bundles, and contributing to a community recipe book. As you work with plants, you’ll reflect on traditional knowledge, self-care, and your relationship with the land. Come to a free […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Sea & Story
program_listings.description: Join a plein air painting session where you’ll paint the ocean and waves, contributing to a collective artwork that visually connects each participant’s piece. You’ll also record the sound of ocean waves, capturing movement and rhythm in both visual and auditory forms. This workshop invites you to reflect on the power of water, interconnectedness, and […]

program_listings.title: Land+Lenses: Waves of Words

program_listings.title: The Grove
program_listings.description: Venue Rentals > The GroveThe Grove The museum’s Grove offers a welcoming, scenic location for gatherings that feel relaxed yet special. Whether you’re planning a garden-style reception, a family celebration, or a community event, the space provides room to spread out, enjoy the fresh air, and connect in a setting inspired by art and nature. With flexible arrangements and supportive staff, it’s the perfect place to bring people together.

program_listings.title: The Ray Brock Hall of Fame
program_listings.description: Venue Rentals > The Hall of FameThe Ray Brock Hall of Fame Elevate your next event—literally and figuratively—in our stunning Mezzanine. Overlooking the main exhibition floor, this airy, light-filled space offers a unique vantage point that immerses guests in the museum’s atmosphere while providing a sense of exclusivity and privacy. With its open layout the mezzanine is ideal for receptions, cocktail gatherings, corporate events, intimate dinners, and special celebrations.

program_listings.title: State of Biodiversity 2022
program_listings.description: At the 2022 State of Biodiversity, we looked at how social issues, especially economic and racial inequities, shape the ecology and evolution of wildlife—particularly in mammals and carnivores. Social heterogeneity plays a pivotal role in shaping the ability of cities to support biodiversity. Thus, using a social-ecological and environmental justice lens is critical for climate and wildlife resilience in a human-dominated world.

program_listings.description: May 13, 2026 We invite you to join us for the final installment of the series, “Turning the Immune System Against Breast Cancer,” a live conversation exploring how the immune system can be harnessed to find and disable breast cancer cells, with the goal of developing treatments that provide long-lasting protection against the disease. Click here to view past webinars from the series.
